identity.name: Publish Itt
identity.metaphor: A clean, well-lit desk with a typewriter and a stack of organized notes.

typography.instructions: Use Playfair Display for all major headings (h1, h2, h3) to give an editorial feel. Use Inter for UI elements, buttons, and long-form writing (for readability). Use JetBrains Mono for word counts, stats, and technical labels.

colors.usage: The background should feel like warm paper (#F9F8F6). Text should be a deep ink color (#2D2A26), never pure black. Use the Accent color (Terracotta/Amber) sparingly for active states, progress bars, or 'New Idea' highlights.

visual_enhancers.textures: Use a subtle grain overlay on the background if possible, or keep it clean matte.

layout_strategy.dashboard: Bento Grid (Mode A). Use a mix of wide cards (Project Overview) and tall cards (Recent Activity).
layout_strategy.manuscript: Distraction-free. Center column (max-w-3xl) for the editor. Collapsible sidebars for chapters and AI tools.
layout_strategy.workflow: Horizontal pipeline. Use 'Step' indicators connected by lines. Status pills should be elegant (outline style).
layout_strategy.spacing: Generous. Use p-8 or p-12 for main containers. Gap-6 or Gap-8 for grids.

components.buttons: Sharp edges (rounded-sm). Primary: Solid Ink (Black/Dark Grey). Secondary: Outline with subtle border.
components.cards: Minimalist. White background (in light mode), 1px subtle border. No heavy shadows. Content is king.
components.inputs: Clean, standard borders. Focus state should be a solid ring of the Primary color.
components.navigation: Sidebar navigation for the 'OS' feel. Collapsible to icons only.
components.progress_bars: Thin, elegant lines. Use the Accent color for the fill.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. UPDATE TAILWIND CONFIG: Extend the theme with the 'Literary Palette' colors. Add 'Playfair Display' to the font family.
2. INSTALL FONTS: Add @import for Playfair Display and Inter/Manrope in index.css.
3. LAYOUT: Create a Sidebar Layout (Left Nav, Main Content). The Sidebar should be collapsible.
4. DASHBOARD: Implement the Bento Grid. Use the 'book_covers' images for the Project cards.
5. MANUSCRIPT: This is the core. Make the editor beautiful. Use a max-width container (prose-lg) centered on the screen. Typography is everything here.
6. WORKFLOW: Use a horizontal stepper. Statuses: Concept -> Outline -> Drafting -> Editing -> Published.
7. ART STUDIO: Use the 'art_prompts' images as examples of what the AI generated. Display them in a masonry grid.
8. COMPONENTS: Customize Shadcn components to match the 'rounded-sm' and 'border-border' aesthetic. No default blue focus rings - use the 'ring' color defined in the palette.
universal_guidelines: Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💹🔢🏆🥇 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
Use sonner for toasts. Sonner component are located in /app/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx
